name: Lambanog Calamansi
description: A bright coconut spirit highball with tart calamansi and a light sweet edge.
originContext: Inspired by common lambanog and citrus serves in the Philippines.
methodSteps: Add lambanog, calamansi, and syrup over ice.
Stir gently and let dilution settle.
estimateNote: Lambanog ABV varies widely; 40% is a working estimate.

name: Gin Pomelo
description: A simple gin and pomelo drink with a soft bittersweet citrus profile.
originContext: Popular home-style Filipino gin mixer pattern.
methodSteps: Pour gin over ice.
Top with pomelo juice and stir.

name: Gin Pineapple
description: Gin lengthened with pineapple juice for a tropical, approachable serve.
methodSteps: Build over ice.
Stir until chilled.

name: Tanduay Rum and Cola
description: Dark rum and cola in a familiar long drink format.
originContext: A Filipino take on rum cola using local rum style.
methodSteps: Pour rum over ice.
Top with cola and stir once.
estimateNote: Rum ABV varies by product; 40% is used for calculation.

name: Tanduay Rum Punch
description: A fruit-forward rum punch with pineapple, orange, and grenadine.
originContext: Party punch style adapted around Filipino rum.
methodSteps: Shake or stir ingredients with ice.
Serve chilled in a tall glass.
estimateNote: A template recipe, not a verified branded formula.

name: Emperador Brandy Cola
description: Brandy and cola with a mellow, sweet profile.
methodSteps: Build over ice.
Stir gently.
estimateNote: Uses 36% ABV as a conservative brandy estimate.

name: Red Horse Beer
description: A strong lager-style beer entry for comparison.
originContext: Popular Philippine beer category represented with estimated ABV.
methodSteps: Serve chilled.
estimateNote: ABV shown as a practical estimate; verify packaging for exact values.

name: San Mig Light

estimateNote: ABV shown as a practical estimate; verify packaging for exact values.

name: Basi
description: Sugarcane wine traditionally associated with northern Luzon.
originContext: Traditional fermented drink with variable strength.
methodSteps: Serve in a small wine-style pour.
estimateNote: Fermentation strength varies; 12% is a midpoint estimate.

name: Tapuy
description: Traditional rice wine with gently sweet fermented notes.
originContext: Traditional Cordilleran rice wine with variable ABV.
methodSteps: Serve in a small pour.
estimateNote: ABV varies by producer and batch; 14% is an educational estimate.

name: Tuba
description: Palm wine with fresh, lightly fermented character.
originContext: Traditional palm sap fermentation, often changing over time.
methodSteps: Serve fresh and chilled when appropriate.
estimateNote: ABV varies with freshness and fermentation time.

name: Weng-weng Style Cocktail
description: A potent mixed-spirit cocktail style shown as an educational high-strength example.
originContext: Inspired by Philippine bar lore around multi-spirit Weng-weng drinks.
methodSteps: Shake with ice or build over ice.
Serve as a high-alcohol example; do not treat as a challenge.
estimateNote: Template only; real recipes vary and may be stronger.

name: Manila Sunshine Style Rum Cocktail
description: A sunny rum, calamansi, pineapple, and orange drink.
methodSteps: Shake or stir with ice.
Serve cold with citrus garnish if desired.
estimateNote: Original educational template using typical 40% ABV rum.
